findings.title: Search Relevance Score Drop
findings.description: The recent deployment (v2.4.1) has resulted in a significant drop in search relevance scores, which could lead to user dissatisfaction and increased support volume. Immediate action is required to analyze and remediate the issue.
assumptions: The drop in relevance is directly related to the new ranking algorithm introduced in v2.4.1.

evidence.excerpt: Search relevance score dropped 15% after deploy v2.4.1

recommendations: Conduct a root-cause analysis of the ranking algorithm.
Consider a rollback if remediation is not feasible.

findings.title: Feature Flag Dependency for Rollback
findings.description: The ability to rollback the v2.4.1 deployment is blocked by a dependent feature flag change. This dependency must be addressed to enable a rollback if necessary.
assumptions: The feature flag is critical for the rollback process.

evidence.excerpt: Rollback was considered but blocked by a dependent feature flag change.

recommendations: Identify and resolve the feature flag dependency to allow for rollback.

findings.title: Increased Support Volume
findings.description: The spike in support volume due to irrelevant search results indicates a potential loss of user trust and engagement. This issue needs to be prioritized for resolution.
assumptions: User dissatisfaction is directly correlated with the relevance issues.

evidence.excerpt: Support volume has spiked 3x since v2.4.1 rolled out.

recommendations: Prioritize the analysis and remediation of the ranking algorithm.

findings.title: Search Latency Optimization
findings.description: The increased search latency on mobile web indicates a potential performance issue with the new ranking model. Profiling and optimization are required to ensure performance meets user expectations.
assumptions: The new ranking model is causing increased latency.

evidence.excerpt: P95 search latency on mobile web has climbed to 890ms.

recommendations: Profile the new ranking model and optimize for mobile performance.

findings.title: Missing Analytics Events for New Query Types
findings.description: The absence of analytics events for new query types (image and voice search) limits visibility into their performance and user adoption. This needs to be addressed to inform future improvements.
assumptions: Analytics events are crucial for measuring the success of new features.

evidence.excerpt: No analytics events were instrumented for these new query types.

recommendations: Implement baseline instrumentation for image and voice search analytics.

findings.title: Establish Baseline Metrics for Image Search
findings.description: To effectively measure the success of the newly launched image search feature, baseline metrics need to be established. This will enable meaningful experimentation and success criteria definition.
assumptions: Baseline metrics are necessary for future improvements.

evidence.excerpt: We need to establish: query volume, zero-result rate, click-through rate on image results.

recommendations: Define and implement a strategy for capturing baseline metrics for image search.

findings.title: Stale Autocomplete Suggestions
findings.description: The stale data in autocomplete suggestions is compounding the relevance problem, leading to user frustration. This issue needs to be addressed to improve user experience.
assumptions: Stale data negatively impacts user experience.

evidence.excerpt: Users are seeing suggestions for products that are out of stock.

recommendations: Adjust the cache TTL for autocomplete suggestions to a more appropriate value.

summary: The analysis reveals critical risks related to the recent deployment of the SearchBoost Query Engine, particularly concerning search relevance, support volume, and performance issues. Immediate actions are recommended to address these risks, with a phased delivery plan proposed to prioritize the most impactful changes.
